html:
Summary

The Kamino protocol facilitates a cross-margined lending market, where multiple assets can be borrowed against a common basket of accepted collateral tokens.

{{MERMAID}}

Protocol Infrastructure

Three on-chain account types form the protocol structure:

  • Market Account — Top-level structure that owns all reserve and obligation accounts. Defines a quote currency (typically USD) to value all assets on a common basis using pricing oracles.
  • Reserve Account — Each token has a unique reserve account serving both borrow and collateral roles. Accepts deposits from suppliers and manages liquidity for borrowers.
  • Obligation Account — Each user has one obligation per market tracking all borrow, collateral, and supply positions. Aggregates risk and valuation in the market's quote currency.

New Loans

Borrowing capacity is determined by loan-to-value (LTV) parameters:

  • Each collateral deposit converts to market quote currency
  • Multiplied by its collateral LTV
  • Sum defines maximum borrowable value

Each borrow asset has a risk weight (borrow factor) that rescales its market value before comparison against maximum borrowable value.

Health Monitoring

Unhealthy borrow value = Σ(Collateral value × liquidation LTV)

Health Factor (HF) = Unhealthy borrow value ÷ Adjusted borrow value

  • HF = 1 → liquidation eligible
  • HF > 1 → healthy
  • HF < 1 → actively liquidatable

New loan LTVs are set below liquidation thresholds to buffer against price volatility.

Liquidations & Loss Socialization

  • Liquidators repay borrowed assets for discounted collateral
  • Operates on individual borrow-collateral pairs
  • Cap applies (~20% max per transaction during standard liquidation)
  • Eligibility ends once HF restored above 1

A severe LTV threshold defines insolvency risk level where up to 100% may be liquidatable. If liquidators fail to restore solvency, the protocol may socialize losses via haircuts on depositor claims.

Asset Valuation

Kamino uses on-chain price oracles. Exponent principal tokens (PT-ONyc) use a custom pricing model:

PT price = (1 - (time_to_maturity_seconds × annual_discount_rate / seconds_per_year)) × underlying_price

Current annual discount rate: 25%. The underlying asset for PT-ONyc is ONyc, so the PT price increases predictably toward maturity.

APY Pricing

Supply and borrow APYs are determined by each reserve's utilization rate. Rates increase as utilization rises. The spread between supply and borrow APY is defined by the protocol's take rate.
